proposals.reason: First dedicated standard for ML-based device risk management, directly affecting technical files.

card.x: First technical specification for risk management of ML-based medical devices. Covers algorithmic bias, model robustness, training data traceability, and post-deployment monitoring.

proposals.reason: Regulation immediately affects certification audits for Class IIb/III devices with algorithms.

card.x: New audit requirements for Class IIb/III devices with algorithms. NBs must explicitly document evaluation of software lifecycle and post-market surveillance robustness.

proposals.reason: Updated methodologies affect clinical trial and observational study protocols using personal data.

card.x: Clarified requirements for health data reuse in research: consent, information, retention periods, pseudonymization, and technical security measures. Mandatory for submissions after 23 May.

proposals.reason: Report identifies recurring gaps in AI device submissions, useful for both UK and EU manufacturers.

card.x: Evaluation of ten AI devices reveals common gaps: insufficient clinical validation, lack of training data transparency, and absence of continuous performance monitoring plans.

proposals.reason: Mandatory traceability requirements now in force for Swiss and Australian markets.

card.x: Swissmedic mandates Swissdamed database for all devices. TGA requires ARTG registration and faster adverse event reporting for Class II+ devices. Both now enforceable.
